audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:S + uống + N]]. Use uống before a drink to say what someone drinks.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:S + uống + N]]: Uống is introduced as a transitive liquid-consumption predicate with a nominal complement.

audienceSections.normal:
#### S + uống + N

Use uống before a drink to say what someone drinks. Example: [[grammar:Tôi uống nước cam.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### S + uống + N

Uống is introduced as a transitive liquid-consumption predicate with a nominal complement. Literal example: [[grammar:Tôi uống nước cam.]]

audienceSections.normal: Put a drink directly after `uống`: `uống nước cam`. Here `cốc` means the cup or glass itself. You will learn how containers can help count amounts later.
audienceSections.expert: Uống takes drink nouns directly. Cốc is an ordinary container noun here; its use as a counting/measure expression is deferred. The grammar IDs remain distinct and cumulative.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Tôi uống nước cam.]] — I drink orange juice.
- [[grammar:Tôi uống nước chanh.]] — I drink lemonade.
- [[grammar:Nước cam lạnh.]] — The orange juice is cold.
- [[grammar:Nước chanh lạnh.]] — The lemonade is cold.
- [[grammar:Tôi có một cốc.]] — I have a cup.
- [[grammar:Tôi chọn nước dừa.]] — I choose coconut water.
- [[grammar:Nước dừa không nóng.]] — The coconut water is not hot.
- [[grammar:Quán cà phê có trà.]] — The café has tea.
- [[grammar:Tôi cũng uống trà.]] — I also drink tea.
- [[grammar:Vâng.]] — Yes.

- [[grammar:Tôi uống nước chanh.]] — Literal reading evidence at Dialogue > line 2.

- [[grammar:Nước chanh lạnh.]] — Literal reading evidence at Dialogue > line 4.

- [[grammar:Tôi chọn nước dừa.]] — Literal reading evidence at Dialogue > line 6.
- [[grammar:Nước dừa không nóng.]] — Literal reading evidence at Dialogue > line 7.
- [[grammar:Quán cà phê có trà.]] — Literal reading evidence at Dialogue > line 8.
- [[grammar:Tôi cũng uống trà.]] — Literal reading evidence at Dialogue > line 9.
